name: Fleysher Law Bankruptcy & Debt Attorneys - Jacksonville Office
description_short: Fleysher Law is a Florida-based bankruptcy law firm offering Chapter 7, Chapter 13, and Chapter 11 filing services with 15+ years of experience through its Jacksonville office.
description_long:
Fleysher Law Bankruptcy & Debt Attorneys operates across Florida with a dedicated Jacksonville office, founded by Emil J. Fleysher, who brings 15+ years of bankruptcy law experience. The firm positions itself as a full-service bankruptcy provider for individuals, families, and small businesses facing overwhelming debt, foreclosure, wage garnishment, and creditor lawsuits.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy services including Chapter 7 liquidation, Chapter 13 repayment plans, Chapter 11 business bankruptcy, and estate planning. They provide free initial consultations, 24-hour emergency assistance, and claim to tailor each case to clients' specific financial situations and goals. The website emphasizes asset protection, understanding Florida-specific exemptions (including homestead protections), and leveraging the automatic stay to halt foreclosures, lawsuits, and wage garnishment immediately upon filing.

Fleysher Law distinguishes itself through localized expertise in Florida bankruptcy law and exemptions, author transparency (Emil J. Fleysher personally fact-checks content), and emphasis on preventing common filing mistakes such as incomplete documentation, wrong exemption claims, fraud, filing under the wrong chapter, and missing mandatory credit counseling. The firm serves multiple Florida markets including Deerfield Beach, Port Saint Lucie, Cape Coral, Orlando, Tampa, and others, suggesting regional scale.

Key limitations include minimal online reviews or ratings visible on the website, no specific case results or success rates published, and limited detail about fee structures or payment options. The website content, while detailed on bankruptcy concepts, reads partially as educational material rather than providing specific case examples or client testimonials beyond their mention of a "Testimonials" page section. Prospective clients should verify current licensing, malpractice history, and request detailed fee estimates before engagement.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: 15+ years bankruptcy law experience with founding partner Emil J. Fleysher directly overseeing case quality
Covers all major bankruptcy chapters (7, 13, 11) plus estate planning in single firm
Emphasizes Florida-specific exemptions and homestead protection laws that maximize asset retention
Offers 24-hour emergency assistance for urgent situations like pending foreclosures
Free initial consultation to assess financial situation before committing to legal fees
Multi-office presence across Florida (Jacksonville, Orlando, Tampa, and 7+ other cities) for accessibility
Educational approach on website explains automatic stay, exemptions, and common filing errors upfront
cons: No published fee structure, payment plans, or cost transparency on website—requires direct contact
No visible client testimonials, case results, or success metrics to evaluate track record
Website content is primarily educational/informational rather than demonstrating specific case outcomes
No information on bar association ratings, malpractice history, or disciplinary records provided
Limited detail on Chapter 11 business bankruptcy expertise despite offering it as a service
best_for: Florida homeowners facing foreclosure who need to preserve equity under state homestead exemptions
Individuals with wage garnishment or active creditor lawsuits seeking immediate automatic stay protection
Small business owners considering Chapter 11 reorganization with access to multi-office legal team
Debt-burdened consumers unsure whether Chapter 7 liquidation or Chapter 13 repayment fits their situation
diagnosis: Fleysher Law is best for Florida residents (particularly homeowners) needing specialized state-specific bankruptcy counsel with multi-office accessibility and emphasis on asset protection. Primary caveat: no fee transparency, published outcomes, or third-party ratings visible online—verification of licensing and direct cost discussion required before engagement.
services: Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing and repayment plan structuring
Chapter 11 business bankruptcy for small businesses
Creditor protection and automatic stay implementation
Asset exemption planning under Florida law
Foreclosure defense and homestead exemption maximization
Credit counseling and debtor education navigation
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
